coreGuarantees: BOLA checkout identity check happens before lock or inventory reads.
Payment capture and payout paths are idempotency-gated before side effects.
Payment and escrow audit records are written before completion events are enqueued.
Payment card and payout bank details never persist as raw values.
Escrow compensation order remains machine-fixed as REFUND_PAYMENT then RESTORE_INVENTORY.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-16 is payment-sensitive and handles checkout, escrow, non-repudiation audit, and payout vault references.

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-16 operating policies. These values may vary by tenant without changing payment security, tenant isolation, or audit ordering.

keys.semantic: How long a checkout cart lock should remain active while payment and inventory reservation complete.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune checkout lock duration for payment provider latency, but the lock must remain finite and idempotency-gated.

keys.semantic: Platform fee applied to marketplace payment splits.

boundedRange.rationale: Fee policy can vary by marketplace contract while the split formula, rounding, and audit guarantees remain fixed.

keys.semantic: Number of days before an eligible escrow hold is scheduled for automatic release.

boundedRange.rationale: Escrow release timing may reflect tenant policy, but dispute state, compensation order, and audit-before-event behavior remain machine-locked.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to payment operating policy values and customer-facing module naming.

edgeCases.name: High-latency payment provider
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A increases the checkout lock TTL for slower payment authorization paths.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T609 still performs BOLA first, then lock acquisition, OCC reservation, audit write, and event enqueue.

edgeCases.name: Contracted platform fee
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A changes platform fee basis points for a negotiated marketplace contract.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T610 still computes seller amount with Math.round, scrubs PII, writes escrow, and appends non-repudiation audit.

edgeCases.name: Longer dispute window
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A extends the escrow auto-release window for high-value transactions.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T611 still keeps dispute updates non-destructive and compensation order machine-fixed.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T609 through T612 remain fixed.
BOLA checkout identity verification must happen before cart lock or inventory reads.
Cart lock and payout lock must happen before downstream side effects.
Inventory reservation must use OCC semantics.
Platform fee formula and rounding remain fixed even when the basis-point value changes.
Payment card and payout bank details are never written raw.
Escrow dispute path never deletes escrow records.
Compensation order remains REFUND_PAYMENT then RESTORE_INVENTORY.
All payment, escrow, and payout events preserve store-before-enqueue ordering.

tenantAEnterpriseMarketplace.description: Tenant A tunes payment operations for enterprise marketplace checkout.
tenantAEnterpriseMarketplace.impact: Checkout has a longer lock window, platform fee reflects contract terms, and escrow releases later while payment security and tenant isolation remain unchanged.
tenantAEnterpriseMarketplace.testable: Fork CI and auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: Raw payment card or payout bank data

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-16 configuration values or display/module metadata.
All four services remain MicroserviceBase subclasses and use TenantContextResolver for tenant scope.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
